scope.summary: Implement the trim OBJ/FBX mesh import pipeline: parse OBJ (and optionally FBX via tailor-fbx-import feature) into TrimMeshV1, compute area-weighted inertia tensor per TR-2.3, assign trim_category, write to tailor_trims with is_library_item flag, and emit TailorTrimImported
acceptance_criteria: OBJ import path parses vertices, face triangles, and normals from a .obj byte stream into TrimMeshV1 without requiring a running ComfyUI or external tool
Inertia tensor computed via area-weighted triangle contribution formula per TR-2.3; det(I) <= 0 produces a hard error before any DB write
Row written to tailor_trims with non-null mesh_json (TrimMeshV1 JSONB) and inertia_tensor_json; a null or empty mesh_json is rejected per TR-6.1.4
TailorTrimImported event emitted to EventLedger on every successful import with trim_id and trim_category
is_library_item flag settable at import time; default FALSE for user-imported custom assets
FBX import path is gated behind the tailor-fbx-import Cargo feature and falls back gracefully (documented error) when the feature is disabled, per the pattern established by R-ANIM-033
scope.risk_if_missed: Custom hardware (belt buckles, armor plates, brand-specific buttons) cannot be imported; only typed primitives (Button, Zipper, Eyelet) defined in code are available; TR-6.1.4 and TailorTrimImported event are dead code paths that the integration test will never exercise

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
